title: OpenCode Project Manifest
description: Machine-readable project configuration for AI agents

description.description: Brief description of what this project does

environments.description: Environment hosting configuration. Used by agents for deployment, database access, environment-specific config, and mapping desktop/mobile apps to their backend environments.

host.description: How the dev environment is hosted

services.description: Multiple services running in dev (e.g., web frontend, API server)

command.description: Optional: command to start this service (e.g., 'npm run dev:api')

healthCheck.description: Optional: path to check if service is ready (e.g., '/api/health')

primaryService.description: Name of the primary service (for default health checks, etc.)

databaseUrl.description: Database connection string (use 'envvar:NAME' for env var reference)

staging.description: Staging/preview environment. Maps to a specific git branch, database, and deployment.

branch.description: Git branch that deploys to this environment (e.g., 'main' for staging)

databaseUrl.description: Database connection string (use 'envvar:NAME' for env var reference)

database.description: Database project configuration for this environment. Helps agents understand which database to target.

projectRef.description: Provider-specific project reference (e.g., Supabase project ref like 'rvuylmblhafnryfdlnkj')

projectName.description: Human-readable project name in the provider dashboard (e.g., 'MyApp Staging')

linkedLocally.description: Whether this database is linked to the local CLI (e.g., 'supabase link'). If true, local migrations target this database.

vercelEnvironment.description: Which Vercel environment variables to use for this environment. NOTE: Vercel defaults 'main' branch to Production environment, so staging may need 'preview' setting.

desktop.description: Desktop app configuration that targets this environment

appName.description: Name of the desktop app that uses this environment (e.g., 'MyApp Dev' for staging)

bundleId.description: macOS bundle identifier for this environment's app (e.g., 'com.company.myapp-dev')

production.description: Production environment. Maps to a specific git branch, database, and deployment.

branch.description: Git branch that deploys to this environment (e.g., 'production' or 'main')

databaseUrl.description: Database connection string (use 'envvar:NAME' for env var reference)

database.description: Database project configuration for this environment. Helps agents understand which database to target.

projectRef.description: Provider-specific project reference (e.g., Supabase project ref like 'hiuhimcmmqdeeyhvdkrb')

projectName.description: Human-readable project name in the provider dashboard (e.g., 'MyApp Production')

linkedLocally.description: Whether this database is linked to the local CLI. Usually false for production.

vercelEnvironment.description: Which Vercel environment variables to use for this environment.

desktop.description: Desktop app configuration that targets this environment

appName.description: Name of the desktop app that uses this environment (e.g., 'MyApp' for production)

bundleId.description: macOS bundle identifier for this environment's app (e.g., 'com.company.myapp')

languages.description: Programming languages used (first is primary if not specified)

apps.description: Applications in this project (monorepo or single)

framework.description: Framework name (e.g., nextjs, remix, express, gin, fastapi, electron, tauri, react-native, flutter)

port.description: DEPRECATED: Use devPort in projects.json registry instead. This field is ignored by agents.

platforms.description: Target platforms for this app (e.g., browser, macos, ios). Omit for server-only apps.

runtime.description: Runtime environment for mobile apps (e.g., hermes, jsc for React Native)

webContent.description: How web content is loaded in desktop/mobile apps. bundled: Local HTML/JS in app package (Electron, Tauri). remote: Loads external URL (WebView wrapping web app). hybrid: Mix of both. Required for type='desktop' to determine verification strategy.

remoteUrl.description: Base URL for remote web content (required when webContent='remote' or 'hybrid'). Used by agents to determine verification target. Example: 'https://app.myservice.com'

testing.description: Per-app testing configuration. Falls back to project-level testing config if not specified.

testDir.description: Directory containing tests for this app

configPath.description: Path to testing framework config file

executablePath.description: Paths to packaged app executables for E2E testing (Electron, Tauri, etc.)

devLaunchArgs.description: Args to pass when launching app in dev mode (e.g., ['./main.js', '--enable-logging'])

platformMetadata.description: Platform-specific configuration for signing, notarization, and distribution

signTool.description: Path to signtool.exe or signing configuration

buildDeploy.description: Build-deploy cycle configuration for desktop apps. Declares paths so the agent can automate build → kill → copy → relaunch before Playwright verification. The agent handles the deploy commands — no custom interpolation needed.

buildCommand.description: Command to build the desktop app (e.g., 'pnpm build', 'npm run build:electron'). Run from the project root.

buildOutputApp.description: Path to the built .app bundle, relative to project root (e.g., 'apps/desktop/dist/mac-universal/Helm.app')

testApp.description: Absolute path to the installed test app on macOS (e.g., '/Applications/Helm Preview.app'). The agent will kill, overwrite, and relaunch this app during the deploy step.

autoDeployAfterStory.description: Whether to automatically run the build-deploy cycle after each story/task completes. Default: true.

buildTimeoutMs.description: Build timeout in milliseconds. Electron builds with electron-builder can take 2+ minutes. Default: 300000 (5 minutes).

triggerPaths.description: Glob patterns for files that trigger a rebuild when changed. If no changed files match, the build-deploy step is skipped. For remote webContent apps, only include main process paths (e.g., 'apps/desktop/electron/**') — UI-only changes don't require a rebuild.

client.description: Database client/ORM (e.g., supabase, prisma, drizzle, pgx, sqlalchemy)

alwaysInclude.description: Activities to always run regardless of automatic resolution (e.g., ["security-critic", "exploit-critic"])

neverSkip.description: Activities that cannot be bypassed when they fail (e.g., ["typecheck", "ui-tester-playwright"])

autoGenerate.description: Automatically generate tests for changed code without existing coverage

qualityChecks.description: Enable quality-beyond-correctness checks (visual stability, performance budgets, CLS)

testVerifySettings.description: Controls which automated Playwright invocation points are enabled. All default to true. When true, Playwright runs automatically — no user prompt. When false, that invocation point is skipped silently. Does NOT gate user-invoked workflows (@qa, @ui-test-full-app-auditor) or test file maintenance.

adHocUIVerify_Analysis.description: Ad-hoc mode: automatically run Playwright analysis probe after code changes

adHocUIVerify_StoryTest.description: Ad-hoc mode: automatically write/run Playwright tests for completed tasks

adHocUIVerify_CompletionTest.description: Ad-hoc mode: automatically run holistic Playwright tests covering the full batch of changes at task spec completion

prdUIVerify_Analysis.description: PRD mode: automatically run per-story Playwright verification after implementation

prdUIVerify_StoryTest.description: PRD mode: automatically write/run per-story Playwright tests

prdUIVerify_PRDCompletionTest.description: PRD mode: automatically run holistic Playwright tests at PRD completion

tools.description: Linting tools (e.g., eslint, prettier, biome, golint, ruff)

git.description: Git workflow and branching strategy configuration

autoCommit.description: Whether agents may automatically commit changes. When false, agents MUST NOT run git commit — they should stage changes and report what to commit, but leave the actual commit to the user. This is HARSHLY ENFORCED across all agents.

branchingStrategy.description: Branching strategy: trunk-based (all work on main), github-flow (feature branches → main), git-flow (develop/release/main), release-branches (develop → release/* → main)

developBranch.description: Integration branch for git-flow and release-branches strategies

releaseBranchPattern.description: Pattern for release branches (git-flow and release-branches strategies)

teamSync.description: Team synchronization settings for multi-machine collaboration. When enabled, agents pull before operations and push after commits.

enabled.description: Enable automatic git pull/push for team synchronization

pullBeforeWork.description: Pull latest changes at session start and before/after commits

confirmBeforePush.description: Ask user for confirmation before pushing

pushRetries.description: Number of retry attempts for push failures (network issues)

conflictBehavior.description: How to handle conflicts: stop-and-alert (safest), auto-merge (attempt git merge), stash-and-alert (stash local, pull, alert)

agentWorkflow.description: How agents interact with git branches. See AGENTS.md 'Git Completion Workflow' for the canonical workflow. PRs to requiresHumanApproval branches are allowed but auto-merge is blocked. Default cascade: pushTo → workBranch → defaultBranch.

workBranch.description: Branch agents should work on (e.g., 'staging', 'develop'). Defaults to defaultBranch if not set.

pushTo.description: Branch to push changes to. Defaults to workBranch if not set, which defaults to defaultBranch.

createPrTo.description: Branch to create PRs against. Defaults to defaultBranch if not set.

requiresHumanApproval.description: Branches requiring human approval before merge. Agents CAN create PRs to these branches, but CANNOT auto-merge. Direct push is blocked. Use for production/main branches that need human review before changes land.

dev.description: Command to start development server (e.g., npm run dev)

devServer.description: Development server configuration. NOTE: The port is stored in ~/.config/opencode/projects.json (projects[].devPort), NOT here. This section is for health checks and startup options only.

healthCheck.description: URL path to check if server is ready (e.g., / or /api/health). Combined with port from projects.json to form full URL.

startupTimeout.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for server to become ready

env.description: Environment variables to set when starting the dev server (e.g., DISABLE_RATE_LIMIT=true for testing)

qualityGates.description: Commands that must pass at various stages

postChangeWorkflow.description: Optional override: Mandatory steps Builder must complete after code changes, before committing. If absent, Builder auto-infers from apps[] configuration (desktop+bundled → rebuild+relaunch, desktop+remote → ensure Electron running, web → HMR).

required.description: Whether this step must pass before commit

type.description: Step type: command (run and check exit code), process (start long-running process), playwright-check (browser/Electron verification)

notes.description: Why this step exists or what it does

beforeCommit.description: Whether this workflow must complete before committing

skipConditions.description: Human-readable conditions under which the workflow can be skipped (e.g., 'docs-only changes', 'config-only changes', 'user explicitly says skip verification')

capabilities.description: Project capabilities. Set at bootstrap, updated by agents as new integrations are added. Used to determine which workflow steps are applicable.

api.description: Exposes an API for external consumption

documentation.description: Documentation system configuration (used by docs-writer, support-article-writer)

planning.description: Planner-specific scope guidance that should be considered for every PRD and story.

considerations.description: Project-specific considerations Planner should pass through when writing PRDs (for example permissions, support docs, AI tools, compliance).

id.description: Stable key for the consideration (kebab-case recommended).

label.description: Human-readable consideration name shown in PRD review tables.

required.description: Whether this consideration must be explicitly addressed in relevant PRD stories.

appliesWhen.description: Optional tags describing when this consideration applies (e.g., api, admin-ui, data-access, auth).

scopeQuestions.description: Questions Planner should answer during scope writing.

acceptanceCriteriaHints.description: Suggested acceptance criteria snippets to include when this consideration applies.

defaultStoryFields.description: Optional per-story defaults that downstream tools can map into story metadata.

notes.description: Optional project-specific implementation or policy notes for this consideration.

workflows.description: Project-specific workflow overrides. If not defined, defaults are read from ~/.config/opencode/data/workflow-defaults.json. See that file for current defaults and available steps.

adhoc.description: Steps to run for ad-hoc requests. If not specified, falls back to toolkit defaults in data/workflow-defaults.json.

prd.description: Steps to run for PRD-driven work. If not specified, falls back to toolkit defaults in data/workflow-defaults.json.

services.description: AWS services used (e.g., dynamodb, s3, sqs, lambda, secrets-manager)

clientWrapperPath.description: Path to standard AWS client wrapper (if exists)

localDevelopment.description: Whether AWS services run locally (LocalStack, etc.)

inputValidation.description: Validation library (e.g., zod, yup, joi, class-validator)

authentication.description: Authentication configuration for AI agents (screenshot, E2E, QA) to log in during testing. Configure via /setup-auth command.

method.description: Authentication method used by this project

skill.description: Name of the auth skill to use (e.g., 'auth-supabase-otp'). Auto-derived from method+provider if not specified.

mode.description: fixed = use same test user, dynamic = create new user each time

emailDefault.description: Default test email if env var not set

passwordVar.description: Environment variable containing test password (for email-password method)

verification.description: How to retrieve verification codes (for OTP flows)

lookupBy.description: Column to look up user by (e.g., 'email')

authenticated.description: Page to land on after successful auth

reuseSession.description: Save auth state to file and reuse across tests in same run

headless.description: Headless auth configuration (skip UI, inject session directly for faster tests)

enabled.description: Enable headless auth for faster tests

method.description: How to acquire auth tokens headlessly. 'cli' runs a shell command that outputs tokens.

command.description: CLI command to run (for method: cli). Supports $ENV_VAR expansion (e.g., 'pnpm cli auth:test-token --email $TEST_EMAIL').

responseFormat.description: Format of CLI command output: json (parsed with tokenPath), text (entire stdout is the token), env (KEY=VALUE lines)

tokenPath.description: Dot-path to access token in response (for json format, e.g., 'data.accessToken')

refreshTokenPath.description: Dot-path to refresh token in response (optional, for json format)

sessionStorage.description: Where to inject the token in browser context after CLI acquisition

uiTestPath.description: Path to the one E2E test that exercises full login UI (e.g., 'e2e/auth.spec.ts')

acquisition.description: Human-readable auth acquisition procedure for agents. Read by agents when headless fails or for initial understanding of how auth works in this project.

description.description: One-line summary of how auth works in this project

steps.description: Ordered steps an agent should follow to get an authenticated session

fallbackToUI.description: If headless/CLI fails, fall back to UI-based auth flow using the appropriate auth skill

notes.description: Additional context about the auth system (gotchas, requirements, env vars needed, etc.)

trigger.description: When to run cleanup: manual (script), auto (test teardown), scheduled (cron)

maxAgeHours.description: Delete test users older than this many hours

selectors.description: Custom CSS selectors for auth forms (for custom/generic provider)

vectorization.description: Codebase and database vectorization for semantic search. Enables agents to query project knowledge semantically instead of relying solely on grep/glob.

enabled.description: Whether vectorization is enabled for this project

storage.description: Where to store embeddings: local (LanceDB) or cloud (Pinecone/Weaviate)

embeddingModel.description: Embedding model: auto (OpenAI if available, else Ollama), openai (text-embedding-3-small), voyage (voyage-code-2), ollama (nomic-embed-text)

contextualRetrieval.description: Add contextual descriptions to chunks before embedding. auto = enable for codebases >50k tokens, always/never = force on/off. Uses Claude Haiku.

include.description: Glob patterns for files to include in index

exclude.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude from index. Note: test files are included by default (see indexTests).

chunkStrategy.description: How to split files: ast (semantic boundaries via Tree-sitter) or sliding-window (fixed token windows)

indexTests.description: Whether to index test files. Recommended true for understanding expected behavior.

relationships.description: Code relationship indexing for call graphs and dependencies

callGraph.description: Build call graph to answer 'what calls this function?' and 'what does this function call?'

dependencies.description: Track import/require statements to answer 'what imports this module?'

testMapping.description: Map tests to the code they cover. Enables 'which tests cover this function?' queries.

gitHistory.description: Git history indexing for understanding code intent and changes

enabled.description: Whether to index git commit history and blame data

depth.description: Number of recent commits to index

architectureSummary.description: AI-generated architecture summaries for high-level understanding

enabled.description: Whether to generate architecture summaries for major modules

refreshInterval.description: How often to regenerate architecture summaries

database.description: Database schema and config table indexing

enabled.description: Whether to index database schema and config tables

connection.description: Database connection reference (use 'env:DATABASE_URL' for env var). Never store actual credentials.

enabled.description: Whether to extract and index schema (tables, columns, types, constraints)

configTables.description: Configuration tables to extract sample rows from (e.g., pricing_tiers, feature_flags)

description.description: Human description of what this table contains

sampleRows.description: Number of sample rows to extract, or 'all' for small tables

hybridWeight.description: Weight for semantic vs keyword search (0 = pure keyword, 1 = pure semantic, 0.7 = balanced)

topK.description: Number of results to return from search

enabled.description: Whether to rerank results for improved precision

model.description: Reranking model: cohere (cloud, best quality) or cross-encoder (local, offline)

onGitChange.description: Automatically refresh index on git commit (via post-commit hook)

onSessionStart.description: Check and refresh stale index when agent session starts

maxAge.description: Force refresh if index is older than this (e.g., '24h', '7d')

cloud.description: Cloud vector storage configuration (only used when storage='cloud')

namespace.description: Namespace/index name for this project (defaults to project name)

credentials.description: API credentials references for vectorization services (never store actual keys)

openai.description: OpenAI API key reference for embeddings

anthropic.description: Anthropic API key reference for contextual retrieval

voyage.description: Voyage AI API key reference for embeddings

cohere.description: Cohere API key reference for reranking

workingDir.description: Project-local directory for temporary AI working files (screenshots, intermediate outputs). Must be gitignored.

verification.description: Playwright verification configuration for UI changes. Controls mandatory visual verification before task completion.

mode.description: playwright-required: Builder must verify UI changes with Playwright before marking complete. All projects get full Playwright verification — the no-ui option has been removed.

testBaseUrl.description: Explicit base URL for E2E tests and verification. Overrides automatic URL resolution. Must include protocol (http:// or https://). Example: 'https://staging.myapp.com'. See test-url-resolution skill for resolution priority.

selectorStrategy.description: strict: Tests must use data-testid, agent adds them if missing. flexible: Prefer data-testid, fall back to role/text selectors.

testDir.description: Directory for generated UI verification test scripts (committed to git).

requiredPasses.description: Number of consecutive test passes required before declaring a feature verified. Ensures stability by catching transient/flaky issues.

autoFixLoop.description: Whether to automatically attempt to fix verification failures. When true, Builder classifies failures (prerequisite vs feature) and delegates fixes to @developer.

fixAttemptTimeoutMinutes.description: Maximum time in minutes to allow for each fix attempt during automated fix loop.

runBroaderTestsAfterFix.description: Whether to run related tests after fixing a verification failure. Helps catch regression in adjacent functionality.

criticMode.description: When to run critic during PRD work: strict (after every story), balanced (every 2-3 stories), fast (once at end)

browserVerification.description: DEPRECATED: Use agents.verification.mode instead. Whether UI changes need browser verification.

heartbeatTimeoutMinutes.description: Session heartbeat timeout in minutes. Session is considered abandoned after this period of inactivity.

commitStrategy.description: When to commit: batch-per-session (default), per-todo (after each todo), or manual (user triggers)

multiSession.description: DEPRECATED: Session coordination is now always-on. This field is ignored. Remove from project.json.

autoMerge.description: When to auto-merge PRs. 'off' = human merges, 'on-e2e-pass' = merge after Builder E2E passes, 'on-ci-pass' = merge after GitHub CI passes

requireApproval.description: Whether PR approval is required before auto-merge (only applies when autoMerge is not 'off')

mergeQueue.description: Merge queue configuration for coordinating parallel session merges

enabled.description: Whether to use merge queue (false = legacy behavior, no queue)

autoProcess.description: Automatically process queue when entry is added (vs manual @merge-coordinator)

requireApproval.description: Require PR approval before auto-merge through queue

runTestsAfterRebase.description: Run full test suite after rebasing (slower but safer)

runE2EAfterRebase.description: Run E2E tests after rebasing (requires dev server)

notifyOnComplete.description: Log notification when merge completes or fails

fallbackChains.description: Per-project fallback chain overrides for agent reassignment. Merges with toolkit defaults unless override is true.

override.description: If true, replace toolkit defaults entirely. If false, merge with defaults (project chains take precedence for matching keys).

chains.description: Map of task type to fallback chain

primary.description: Primary agent for this task type

alternatives.description: Alternative agents to try if primary fails (in order)

skills.description: Project-specific skills configuration. Skills are step-by-step guides for common patterns in THIS project.

generated.description: Skills that have been generated for this project

conventions.description: Path to coding conventions documentation (e.g., docs/CONVENTIONS.md)

relatedProjects.description: Companion projects related to this one. Enables agents to find related projects (e.g., documentation site, marketing site, API backend) without guessing based on names.

relationship.description: Relationship type. Common conventions: documentation-site, documented-project, marketing-site, marketed-product, admin-dashboard, managed-service, api-backend, frontend-client, mobile-app, web-counterpart, shared-library, dependent-project, monorepo-sibling, test-harness, tested-system

label.description: Optional label for disambiguation when multiple projects share the same relationship type (e.g., 'user-docs' vs 'api-docs' for two documentation-site relationships)

description.description: Optional human-readable description of this relationship
